fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Louisiana Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Louisiana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity louisiana, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to louisiana, pharmacy technician compact louisiana, louisiana pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: Louisiana Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address: 3718 N. Acadian Thruway E., Suite 200, Baton Rouge, LA 70805

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in Louisiana.

steps.title: 1. Review Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: Before applying, ensure you meet the Louisiana Board of Pharmacy's requirements, including being at least 18 years old, having a high school diploma or equivalent, and demonstrating good moral character. Review the complete list of requirements on the LABP Technician page (labp.org/Content/Technician).

steps.title: 2. Create an Online Account
steps.description: Navigate to the LABP Online Licensing System (labp.org/Online). Click on 'Create an Account' to establish your user profile, then log in to access the application portal.

steps.title: 3. Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Access and complete the Pharmacy Technician Registration application within the LABP Online Licensing System. Provide all required personal information, educational background, and acknowledge the criminal history affidavit.

steps.title: 4. Submit Fingerprints for Background Check
steps.description: Follow the specific instructions provided by the Louisiana Board of Pharmacy, often within the application process, to submit your fingerprints to the Louisiana State Police for mandatory state and federal criminal background checks.

steps.title: 5. Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the required $50 application/registration fee via the online system. Your application will not be processed until the fee is successfully paid.

steps.title: 6. Obtain National Certification (Post-Registration)
steps.description: Upon initial registration approval, you will be able to work as a pharmacy technician. Within 18 months of your initial registration date, you must pass a Board-approved national certification exam (PTCB or NHA ExCPT) to maintain your registration, unless you meet specific exemption criteria (e.g., enrolled in an ACPE-accredited college of pharmacy).

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Louisiana offers a temporary or trainee pharmacy technician permit that allows new applicants to begin working in a pharmacy setting under direct pharmacist supervision while completing their certification requirements. Contact the Louisiana Board of Pharmacy for specific temporary permit restrictions and duration.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Louisiana by submitting a new application to the Louisiana Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Louisiana-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 1 to 2 Weeks.

faqs.question: How much does a Louisiana pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Louisiana have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Louisiana. To work in Louisiana, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Louisiana?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Louisiana while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Louisiana by submitting a new application to the Louisiana Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Louisiana-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 1 to 2 Weeks.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in Louisiana
hero.dek: Apply online through the Louisiana Board of Pharmacy's official LABP Online Licensing System (labp.org/Online). Create an account, locate the Pharmacy Technician application, and follow the instructions to complete your registration.

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the LABP Online Licensing System, click 'Create an Account' to register a new user account. Once your account is established, log in and select the Pharmacy Technician application to begin.
